storyline: MP Materials (MP) is the main US rare-earth miner. It rose on China's rare-earth export curbs — Beijing restricting critical-mineral exports makes a domestic supplier strategically valuable. The catalyst is open-ended (ongoing trade tension, no single resolution date); MP's price flattened around Nov 2025 as the scarcity premium faded. What we'd want: exit when the curbs ease / the premium fades (~Nov 2025). Here the agent held the position live the whole window — the under-exit case, because an open-ended catalyst never cleanly 'resolves' on a date.

seeds.title: The only US rare-earth miner soars as China curbs exports
seeds.snippet: MP Materials (MP) is surging as China restricts rare-earth exports; MP is the lone scaled US producer, a direct reshoring/tariff beneficiary still under-owned.

MP.thesis: China curbs rare-earth exports, lone US scaled producer benefits
MP.exit_case: none — China's export curbs on rare earths remain in force, no reversal reported
MP.assessment: Entry week: China's rare-earth export curbs are a fresh, specific, datable supply shock and MP Materials is explicitly named as the lone scaled US producer benefiting — still under-owned per press framing, not yet mainstream-saturated. No concrete resolution or reversal of the curbs reported; broader tariff volatility this week is noise, not thesis-relevant. Catalyst arc still open: further Chinese licensing actions, US stockpiling/DoD support, or downstream magnet-maker deals remain plausible next legs.
MP.exit_advice: Hold MP; watch for any China licensing rollback or new US refining capacity announcements that could dilute scarcity premium

lifecycle.exit_case: Trump-Xi Busan summit (Oct 30) reportedly produced a truce pausing China's rare-earth export curbs for one year.
lifecycle.assessment: After 24 quiet-to-escalating weeks, the Trump-Xi summit this week (Oct 30, Busan) is the concrete event this journal repeatedly flagged as the next leg - and its outcome (a reported one-year truce pausing China's rare-earth export controls in exchange for the US shelving its threatened 100% tariff) reverses the driving condition that anchored this thesis since April. This is not mere staleness or crowding; it is the specific catalyst's arc reaching resolution via negotiated de-escalation, which the framework requires treating as exit even absent a headline explicitly naming MP. Market coverage this week frames the summit as 'underwhelming' rather than triumphant, but the substance - curbs paused - removes the acute supply-shock premise MP was priced on.
lifecycle.exit_advice: Exit MP: the summit that was the last concrete pending catalyst has now occurred, and reporting indicates China agreed to suspend/pause the rare-earth export curbs as part of a trade truce with eased US tariff threats in return. The specific driving condition - active Chinese export licensing restrictions - has reversed (paused), and the arc that began with the April curbs and escalated through the Oct 17 tightening/tariff-threat leg has now closed with this summit outcome. No further concrete catalyst is scheduled beyond speculative renewal-of-truce risk. Close the position; MP's fundamental scaled-producer status remains but the discrete curb-driven catalyst is resolved.
